    • Orientation Plots with ggplot
    • Statistics
    • Statistics on the Sphere

    • Faults and Paleostress
    • Mohr Circle
    • Other
    • Strain and Vorticity Analysis
    • Deformation and Velocity Gradient Tensor

Uncertainties of direction stress inversion after Yamaji and Sato (2006)

Source: R/stress_inversion_yamaji.R
slip_inversion_yamaji_sato_boot.Rd

Bootstrap resampling to evaluate solution precision (Section 6). Yields B stress tensors from resampled datasets. The dispersion of these tensors on S^5 approximates the noise level of the data (Eq. 37).

Usage

slip_inversion_yamaji_sato_boot(x, weights = NULL, n_boot = 500L)

Arguments

x

object of class "Pair" or "Fault" with at least 4 rows.

weights

numeric. Weightings for the faults. Must have the same length as x

n_boot

integer. Number of bootstrap replicates

Value

list:

optimal

slip_inversion_yamaji_sato() result for the full dataset

thetas

length-B vector of angular stress distances from optimal

dispersion_deg

mean angular stress distance (Theta-bar); approximates the noise level p of the data (Fig. 8 of paper)

sd_deg

standard deviation of Theta values

D_bar

mean Orife-Lisle distance from optimal

DM_bar

mean Michael distance from optimal

See also

slip_inversion_yamaji_sato()

Examples

slip_inversion_yamaji_sato_boot(angelier1990$AVB)
#> $optimal
#> $optimal$stress_tensor
#>            [,1]       [,2]      [,3]
#> [1,] -0.5953283  0.1737441 0.3615258
#> [2,]  0.1737441 -0.4261257 0.2223252
#> [3,]  0.3615258  0.2223252 1.0214540
#> 
#> $optimal$principal_axes
#> Line object (n = 3):
#>          azimuth    plunge
#> sigma1  36.56594 74.346683
#> sigma2 246.92410 13.592506
#> sigma3 155.07122  7.616779
#> 
#> $optimal$principal_vals
#>     sigma1     sigma2     sigma3 
#>  1.1399309 -0.4105345 -0.7293963 
#> 
#> $optimal$principal_faults
#> Fault object (n = 2):
#>      dip_direction       dip  azimuth   plunge sense
#> [1,]      162.2618  68.95608 126.3346 64.58613     1
#> [2,]      146.7537 126.14052 354.0366 50.58977     1
#> 
#> $optimal$stress_shape
#> $optimal$stress_shape$R
#> [1] 0.8294243
#> 
#> $optimal$stress_shape$phi
#> [1] 0.1705757
#> 
#> $optimal$stress_shape$bott
#> [1] 1.205656
#> 
#> $optimal$stress_shape$A_phi
#> [1] 0.1705757
#> 
#> $optimal$stress_shape$type
#> [1] "N"
#> 
#> 
#> $optimal$tau_mean
#> [1] 0.6001158
#> 
#> $optimal$stress_components
#>             normal       shear slip_tendency dilatation_tendency
#>  [1,] -0.641267976  0.39462510   -0.61538251           0.9528556
#>  [2,] -0.455183853  0.64135736   -1.40900727           0.8533095
#>  [3,] -0.525560757  0.54188332   -1.03105742           0.8909578
#>  [4,] -0.428455701  0.67411940   -1.57337013           0.8390113
#>  [5,] -0.064363808  0.83837065  -13.02549799           0.6442396
#>  [6,] -0.166412315  0.85745223   -5.15257679           0.6988307
#>  [7,] -0.357348406  0.73957152   -2.06960912           0.8009723
#>  [8,] -0.375258185  0.72027866   -1.91942159           0.8105531
#>  [9,] -0.688606648  0.26006313   -0.37766572           0.9781795
#> [10,] -0.680413941  0.27816766   -0.40882122           0.9737968
#> [11,] -0.714240026 -0.06954651    0.09737134           0.9918921
#> [12,] -0.679445362  0.29957839   -0.44091609           0.9732786
#> [13,] -0.652785795  0.31832994   -0.48764839           0.9590170
#> [14,] -0.524116602  0.58156231   -1.10960483           0.8901852
#> [15,]  0.253590889  0.93304924    3.67934844           0.4741492
#> [16,] -0.659793682  0.18051805   -0.27359772           0.9627659
#> [17,] -0.542109362  0.51698469   -0.95365387           0.8998105
#> [18,] -0.506321094  0.60593591   -1.19674237           0.8806655
#> [19,] -0.326811101  0.76304867   -2.33483095           0.7846363
#> [20,] -0.285320602  0.78855966   -2.76376699           0.7624409
#> [21,]  0.198494647  0.93170746    4.69386696           0.5036230
#> [22,]  0.187381302  0.93445276    4.98690506           0.5095681
#> [23,]  0.240103463  0.93016098    3.87400070           0.4813643
#> [24,]  0.820112941  0.69976180    0.85325053           0.1710872
#> [25,] -0.245871474  0.81732539   -3.32419772           0.7413375
#> [26,] -0.299901771  0.77169302   -2.57315258           0.7702411
#> [27,] -0.470674957  0.62988042   -1.33824928           0.8615965
#> [28,] -0.665506152  0.32961084   -0.49527842           0.9658218
#> [29,] -0.619687003  0.40155461   -0.64799585           0.9413108
#> [30,] -0.410116610  0.62896249   -1.53361867           0.8292007
#> [31,] -0.246030052  0.80363642   -3.26641567           0.7414223
#> [32,] -0.001496547  0.88934457 -594.26454353           0.6106087
#> [33,] -0.725878252  0.04861919   -0.06697982           0.9981180
#> 
#> $optimal$misfit
#> $optimal$misfit$alpha
#>  [1]  1.1202493  2.0536177  4.1593140 15.7822083 17.7699860  5.7906986
#>  [7]  3.1403013 14.8381427  0.1566777  3.7763224 79.1502359  1.5535490
#> [13]  1.6879700 11.6802965 10.8548707 40.2293170  5.7417792  3.2054355
#> [19]  0.6667046  9.0672886  6.0849437  0.1002293  9.2220042  3.8248192
#> [25] 15.9914523  5.5236375  6.7580359 14.9662354 14.0075329 16.7214363
#> [31] 16.8812196  2.3298302 22.9927797
#> 
#> $optimal$misfit$alpha_mean
#> [1] 11.14634
#> 
#> $optimal$misfit$rup
#>  [1]  54.448617  26.125136  37.866444  32.831663  30.560405  10.100874
#>  [7]  15.454931  28.946922  69.970657  67.982628 101.817554  65.427104
#> [13]  63.267640  36.837994  21.105546  85.156850  41.040137  30.394856
#> [19]  11.940788  17.537911  13.369792   7.903400  18.234399  20.114063
#> [25]  27.605154  14.191574  29.061924  63.990275  56.144452  36.925869
#> [31]  29.182800   4.922226  94.857305
#> 
#> $optimal$misfit$rup_mean
#> [1] 38.34297
#> 
#> $optimal$misfit$quality
#>  [1] acceptable good       good       good       good       good      
#>  [7] good       good       acceptable acceptable poor       acceptable
#> [13] acceptable good       good       poor       good       good      
#> [19] good       good       good       good       good       good      
#> [25] good       good       good       acceptable acceptable good      
#> [31] good       good       poor      
#> Levels: good acceptable poor
#> 
#> $optimal$misfit$quality_summary
#>       n_good n_acceptable       n_poor 
#>           23            7            3 
#> 
#> $optimal$misfit$flipped
#>  [1] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE FALSE
#> [13] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
#> [25] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
#> 
#> 
#> $optimal$SHmax
#> [1] 65.07122
#> 
#> $optimal$alpha
#>  [1]  1.1202493  2.0536177  4.1593140 15.7822083 17.7699860  5.7906986
#>  [7]  3.1403013 14.8381427  0.1566777  3.7763224 79.1502359  1.5535490
#> [13]  1.6879700 11.6802965 10.8548707 40.2293170  5.7417792  3.2054355
#> [19]  0.6667046  9.0672886  6.0849437  0.1002293  9.2220042  3.8248192
#> [25] 15.9914523  5.5236375  6.7580359 14.9662354 14.0075329 16.7214363
#> [31] 16.8812196  2.3298302 22.9927797
#> 
#> $optimal$mean_alpha
#> [1] 11.14634
#> 
#> $optimal$y
#> [1] -0.4209607 -0.3013164  0.7222770  0.2223252  0.3615258  0.1737441
#> 
#> $optimal$method
#> [1] "yamaji_sato"
#> 
#> 
#> $thetas
#>   [1]  4.344583  3.754088 36.999349  8.723584  9.947542 14.259610 24.283674
#>   [8]  3.095480 14.406892  4.616729 22.577845 16.592530 31.051414  7.959735
#>  [15] 29.177293  7.959168 24.553593 10.010926 20.274598 22.001559 30.659134
